columns.name: Timestamp
columns.description: The date and time of the Web traffic event, expressed as a UTC-formatted string.

columns.name: PolicyIdentityLabel
columns.description: The identity that made the request.

columns.name: InternalClientIp
columns.description: The internal IP address of the computer making the request.

columns.name: ExternalClientIp
columns.description: The egress IP address of the network where the request originated.

columns.name: DestinationIp
columns.description: The destination IP address of the request.

columns.name: ContentType
columns.description: The type of web content, typically text/html.

columns.name: Action
columns.description: Whether the request was allowed or blocked.

columns.name: Url
columns.description: The URL requested.

columns.name: Referer
columns.description: The referring domain or URL.

columns.name: UserAgent
columns.description: The browser agent that made the request.

columns.name: StatusCode
columns.description: The HTTP status code; should always be 200 or 201.

columns.name: RequestSize
columns.description: Request size in bytes.

columns.name: ResponseSize
columns.description: Response size in bytes.

columns.name: ResponseBodySize
columns.description: Response body size in bytes.

columns.name: Sha256Hash
columns.description: The hex digest of the response content.

columns.name: Categories
columns.description: The security categories for this request, such as Malware.

columns.name: AvDetections
columns.description: The detection name according to the antivirus engine used in file inspection.

columns.name: Puas
columns.description: A list of all potentially unwanted application (PUA) results for the proxied file as returned by the antivirus scanner.

columns.name: AmpDisposition
columns.description: The status of the files proxied and scanned by Cisco Advanced Malware Protection (AMP) as part of the File Inspection feature; can be Clean, Malicious or Unknown.

columns.name: AmpMalwareName
columns.description: If Malicious, the name of the malware according to AMP.

columns.name: AmpScore
columns.description: The score of the malware from AMP. This field returns blank ("") unless the verdict is Unknown, in which the value will be 0.

columns.name: PolicyIdentityType
columns.description: The first identity type that made the request. Examples: Roaming Computer, Network.

columns.name: BlockedCategories
columns.description: The category that resulted in the destination being blocked.

columns.name: Identities
columns.description: All identities associated with this request.

columns.name: IdentityTypes
columns.description: The type of identities that were associated with the request. Examples: Roaming Computer, Network.

columns.name: RequestMethod
columns.description: The HTTP request method. Examples: GET, POST, HEAD, PUT, DELETE.

columns.name: DlpStatus
columns.description: If the request was Blocked for DLP.

columns.name: CertificateErrors
columns.description: Any certificate or protocol errors in the request.

columns.name: FileName
columns.description: The name of the file.

columns.name: RulesetId
columns.description: The ID number assigned to the ruleset.

columns.name: RuleId
columns.description: The ID number assigned to the rule.

columns.name: DestinationListIds
columns.description: The ID number assigned to a destination list.

columns.name: IsolateAction
columns.description: The remote browser isolation state associated with the request.

columns.name: FileAction
columns.description: The action taken on a file in a remote browser isolation session.

columns.name: WarnStatus
columns.description: The Warn page's state associated with the request.

columns.name: ForwardingMethod
columns.description: The method used to forward the proxy events. Example: Secure Web Appliance.

columns.name: Producer
columns.description: The producer of the proxy events.

columns.name: MspOrganizationId
columns.description: The Secure Access parent organization ID.

columns.name: GeoLocationOfBlockedDestinationCountries
columns.description: The ISO-3166 IDs of one or more countries where destination IPs blocked by policy are located.

columns.name: ApplicationIds
columns.description: The ID of the destination application.

columns.name: HostName
columns.description: The hostname of the user device.

columns.name: DataCenter
columns.description: The name of the data center that processed the user-generated traffic.

columns.name: Egress
columns.description: TRUE indicates that the egress IP was a reserved IP.

columns.name: ServerName
columns.description: The name of the server according to the TLS protocol server name indication (SNI), if present, or from the server's SAN certificate common name (CN).

columns.name: TimeBasedRule
columns.description: TRUE indicates that a the rule was applied due to a time condition.

columns.name: SecurityOverridden
columns.description: TRUE indicates that security filtering was explicitly overridden and not applied during enforcement.

columns.name: DetectedResponseFileType
columns.description: The file type that resulted in a blocked response. Examples: exe, avi.

columns.name: WarnCategories
columns.description: The ID of one or more content categories in lists matched for a Warn action by the rule.

columns.name: OrganizationId
columns.description: The Secure Access organization ID.

columns.name: ApplicationEntityName
columns.description: It refers to the specific name of an application entity within a system.

columns.name: ApplicationEntityCategory
columns.description: It represents the classification grouping of application entities based on shared characteristics or functions.

columns.name: EgressIp
columns.description: The public IP address assigned to a session as it exits the Secure Access ZTA infrastructure en route to the destination application.

columns.name: AiModelName
columns.description: Indicates the name of the AI model involved in the transaction or event.

columns.name: AiSupplyChainCategories
columns.description: The list of AI supply chain categories associated with the event.

columns.name: EventCorrelationId
columns.description: A unique identifier generated for each network request, the Event Correlation ID stitches together all related events across various security services (Firewall, SWG, ZTNA) to provide a unified, end-to-end view of a single traffic flow.
